record.bridgyOriginalText:
Don't mind me, just venting now.

The thing that really grinds my gears is that we're defunding the fine folk who predict storms at the same time that #ClimateChange is making the whole system more chaotic.

It's absolutely, 100%, the plan - we "can't prove" woke shit without data. Therefore, the climate is fine. QED. 🙄

I've lived in MN since I was 12. Over the past 35-ish years I've watched our winters change so drastically that we've changed which garden zone we're in.

I lived in a lot of places as a kid. One of my most vivid early childhood memories is seeing mountains on 3 sides of us on fire while we hosed down the roof every few hours. 'Cause of the hot ashes floating down from miles away.

When I see CA wildfires on TV these days - it's nuts - and I don't think I could live there again unless I lived in an RV. Seriously. I'd want to be able to drive my house the fuck out of town in a moments notice.

So anyway, 2 tornados touched down within striking distance of my home today. Luckily nothing on my tiny property seems damaged & we didn't lose power. Our cars are safe in the garage. But people and property were probably harmed elsewhere in my community. And I expect that "tornado alley" is expanding to include us. Dammit.

Thank you for coming to my TED Bitch-N-Moan, I guess.
